name: browser_navigate
title: Open URL
description: Open a URL (http/https) in the dock.

name: browser_back
title: Back
description: Go back.

name: browser_forward
title: Forward
description: Go forward.

name: browser_reload
title: Reload
description: Reload the page.

name: browser_snapshot
title: Page snapshot
description: Read the page: interactive elements (with stable refs to click by) + visible text + url/title.

name: browser_screenshot
title: Screenshot
description: PNG screenshot of the dock (data URL).

name: browser_click
title: Click
description: Click an element by its snapshot ref (or a CSS selector).

name: browser_type
title: Type text
description: Type text into an input by ref/selector.

name: browser_scroll
title: Scroll
description: Scroll the page vertically by dy pixels (relative), or to the absolute offset dy when to='y'.

name: browser_select
title: Select option
description: Set a <select> value by ref/selector.

name: browser_eval
title: Evaluate JS
description: Evaluate a JS expression in the page and return its JSON result.

name: browser_console
title: Console tail
description: Recent console lines (tail).

name: browser_network_failures
title: Network failures
description: Recent failed network requests (tail).

name: browser_wait_for
title: Wait for selector
description: Wait until a selector appears (or timeout).

name: browser_tab_list
title: List tabs
description: List the open tabs in this workspace's browser (index, url, title) and which is active.

name: browser_tab_new
title: Open a tab
description: Open a new tab, optionally navigating it to a url. Returns the tab list.

name: browser_tab_select
title: Switch tab
description: Switch the active tab by index (from browser_tab_list) or tab id. Returns the tab list.

name: list_panes
title: List panes
description: List the live panes in this workspace's fleet: id, size, state (idle/busy/attention), swarm role, remote host, and title.

name: capture_pane
title: Capture pane
description: Read a pane's scrollback tail (default 1000 lines, max 10000). The text is returned to you and goes nowhere else.

name: mail_read
title: Read mailbox
description: Read the swarm mailbox as yourself (your pane identity): messages addressed to you or to all, with id greater than since.

since.description: Return messages with id greater than this (default 0).

name: list_owners
title: List file owners
description: List the live file-ownership claims: which pane owns which repo-relative glob pattern.

name: list_board
title: List board cards
description: List YOUR project's board: the board meta (id, name, bound repo) and its live cards — id, title, lane (backlog/todo/doing/review/done), notes, position order, priority, labels, blocked, revision, and the working pane when one is bound. Your pane sees exactly the board of the project it works in.

name: get_card
title: Get one card
description: Read one card in full, plus its recent activity log (who did what: human, panes, queue, sync). Use the card's `revision` for a safe conditional update.

name: report_working_directory
title: Report working directory
description: Declare this pane's primary working directory to MoggingLabs. Call this at session start and immediately whenever you switch the primary checkout or worktree. Pass an absolute path; do not report transient subprocess directories. This tool is self-scoped to your pane and never needs a workspace write grant.

path.description: Absolute path of the primary directory or worktree where this agent is working.

name: send_to_pane
title: Send to pane
description: Type text into another pane's terminal and press Enter (set noEnter to skip the Enter). Identical to `mogging send`.

name: send_key
title: Press key
description: Press one named key in a pane — a closed allowlist; escapes are never synthesized from arguments. Identical to `mogging send-key`.

key.description: A key name from the allowlist.

name: mail_send
title: Send mail
description: Send a mailbox message to one pane id or 'all', as yourself (your pane identity travels as the sender). Body max 16 KB. Identical to `mogging mail send`.

name: claim_files
title: Claim files
description: Claim exclusive ownership of a repo-relative glob for your pane. An overlapping claim is DENIED with the owning pane named. Identical to `mogging claim`.

name: release_files
title: Release files
description: Release your pane's file claims — one pattern, or all of them with all=true. Identical to `mogging release`.

all.description: Release every claim this pane holds.

name: update_card
title: Update board card
description: Patch a card on your project's board: move it (column and/or before another card), and/or set title, notes, priority, labels, blocked. Pass expectedRevision (from list_board/get_card) to refuse-instead-of-clobber when the card changed under you. A card being worked by ANOTHER live pane refuses with its owner named.

note.description: New note text for the card.

blockedReason.description: Why it is blocked (shown on the card).

before.description: Reorder: place before this card id (same column).

expectedRevision.description: Refuse the write unless the card's revision still equals this.

name: create_card
title: Create board card
description: Create a card on your project's board (default column: todo). Returns the card, including its id and revision.

name: claim_card
title: Claim board card
description: Claim a card as YOUR working item: binds it to your pane so other agents' writes refuse with you named. Refused if another LIVE pane already works it. Release with release_card when done.

name: release_card
title: Release board card
description: Release a card you claimed (your pane stops being its working pane). Refused unless your pane holds it.

name: comment_card
title: Comment on card
description: Append a progress comment to a card's activity log (max 2 KB). Comments are LOCAL — they live with the card on this machine and nowhere else.

name: archive_card
title: Archive board card
description: Archive a card: it leaves the lanes but stays stored and restorable. Agents archive — deleting is a human verb. Refused while another live pane works the card.

name: brain_status
title: Code-graph status
description: Read the code-graph brain's status for YOUR project: indexed files/nodes/edges and languages, the index generation, whether it is dirty (changes seen but not yet absorbed), reference-resolution fidelity (resolvedRefs vs droppedRefs — dropped means ambiguous, honestly uncounted as edges), and parse-cache stats. Every brain answer is stamped { generation, dirty, root } so you always know how fresh the map is. Free to call; scoped to the checkout your pane works in.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane; inside a pane your own checkout is resolved for you.

name: query_graph
title: Query code graph
description: List graph nodes (modules, classes, interfaces, types, functions, methods, enums, consts) filtered by kind, name glob, and/or file-path glob (* and ? wildcards). Paged: default 50 per page, max 200 — pass the returned `cursor` to continue; `truncated` is set whenever more remained. Results come from YOUR checkout's partition unless scope="project" (then every hit is labeled with its checkout root).

name.description: Name filter — exact, or a glob like handle* or *Store.

file.description: Root-relative file-path filter — exact, or a glob like src/*.ts.

cursor.description: Continue a previous page (opaque; from the last answer).

scope.description: "checkout" (default) = your own checkout only; "project" = every checkout/worktree of the project, hits labeled by root.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: get_node
title: Get graph node
description: Read one graph node in full by id (from query_graph / find_symbol): kind, name, file, start/end lines, its signature line, and fileHash — the sha256 of its file as last indexed, which is the expectedFileHash a symbol write needs. Unknown or out-of-scope ids answer a typed unknown-node refusal, never a guess.

scope.description: "checkout" (default) or "project" — a sibling worktree's node resolves only under project scope.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: get_neighbors
title: Get node neighbors
description: Walk one hop from a node: incoming, outgoing, or both edge directions, optionally filtered to edge kinds (comma-separated from: defines, imports, references, extends, implements). Each neighbor carries the connecting edge's kind and direction. Capped and truncation-flagged, never silently trimmed.

kinds.description: Comma-separated edge kinds to include (default: all).

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: shortest_path
title: Shortest graph path
description: Breadth-first shortest path between two nodes over the code graph (edges walked in both directions), answering the node/edge chain when one exists within the caps. Depth cap maxDepth (default 8) and a visited cap guard the walk — exceeding either answers a typed too-deep refusal; two genuinely unconnected nodes answer found:false.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: find_symbol
title: Find symbol definition
description: Answer "what defines X": definition nodes matching a name — exact match first; when nothing matches exactly the name is retried as a glob. Optionally restricted to one kind. Returns each definition with its file, lines, and signature.

name.description: Symbol name — exact, or a glob like parse*.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: find_references
title: Find references
description: Answer "what references X": the RESOLVED incoming reference edges to a node (by id, or by exact definition name), each with the referencing node and its file. Honesty note: when the last index dropped ambiguous references (droppedRefs nonzero), the answer says so — the list is the resolved truth, never a guess at the ambiguous remainder.

id.description: Target node id (preferred when you have it).

name.description: Exact definition name — used when id is not given.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: get_repo_map
title: Get repo map
description: A ranked map of the project you are working in: the most-referenced files first, each with its definition SIGNATURES (never file bodies), greedily filled into a character budget. Ranking is PageRank over the code graph — symbols referenced from many distinct files float to the top. The last line is the honesty stamp: [repomap: generation N, X/Y files] — what you see, out of what exists, as of which index generation. Call this first in an unfamiliar checkout.

budget.description: Character budget, 200-16000 (default 4000). Characters, not tokens — deterministic across CLIs.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane; inside a pane your own checkout is resolved for you.

name: list_libraries
title: List project libraries
description: The project's third-party dependencies as the LOCKFILES pin them: name, exact version (or the manifest's range, honestly flagged pinned:false when nothing pins), whether it is a direct dependency, whether the pinned version is installed on disk, and whether distilled docs are cached for it. Ecosystems: npm, py, go, cargo. Direct deps first; the list is capped with truncation flagged. Use get_library_docs for a dep's README and type signatures.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: get_library_docs
title: Get library docs
description: Version-correct docs for ONE project dependency (from list_libraries): the README and distilled type signatures of the version actually pinned and installed, read from disk — never a guess about some other release. Every answer stamps { version, source: 'disk' | 'registry' }; an unversioned answer does not exist. topic filters README sections by heading. When the pinned version is not on disk, pass fetch:true to allow ONE registry fetch (npm/PyPI JSON, HTTPS, pinned version, size-capped) — that path needs this workspace's library-docs consent (default OFF; the human grants it in Settings) and refuses with reason 'consent' otherwise.

name.description: Dependency name exactly as list_libraries reports it (scoped npm names like @scope/pkg included).

ecosystem.description: Disambiguate when one name exists in two ecosystems.

topic.description: Keep only README sections whose heading matches this (case-insensitive).

fetch.description: Allow ONE consent-gated registry fetch when the pinned version's docs are not on disk.

root.description: Absolute project root — ONLY for sessions outside a pane.

name: replace_symbol_body
title: Replace symbol body
description: Replace one symbol's ENTIRE definition (its start line through its end line, declaration included) with new source, in your own checkout only. Compare-and-swap: pass expectedFileHash (the fileHash from get_node) — if the file's bytes changed since, the write refuses with reason 'stale' carrying the fresh hash; re-query the node and retry with current lines. The write lands atomically, the file is re-indexed before the reply, and the answer carries the new generation, the symbol's node (when it still resolves), and newFileHash for your next edit. Body text lands as INERT BYTES, verbatim; a body without a trailing newline gets the file's own line ending appended.

id.description: Node id of the symbol to replace (from find_symbol / get_node).

expectedFileHash.description: sha256 of the file's current bytes — get_node answers it as fileHash.

body.description: The symbol's full new definition, max 64 KB. Bring your own indentation — lines land verbatim.

name: insert_after_symbol
title: Insert after symbol
description: Insert whole lines immediately AFTER a symbol's last line, in your own checkout only. Indentation is taken from the symbol's first line and prepended to every non-empty line of text — write the text unindented. Same compare-and-swap as replace_symbol_body: expectedFileHash must match the file's current bytes (get_node answers it as fileHash), a mismatch refuses 'stale' with the fresh hash, and the reply carries the new generation, the anchor node, and newFileHash.

id.description: Anchor node id — the new lines land after its end line.

expectedFileHash.description: sha256 of the file's current bytes — get_node answers it as fileHash.

text.description: Whole lines to insert, unindented, max 64 KB — the anchor's indentation is applied for you.

name: insert_before_symbol
title: Insert before symbol
description: Insert whole lines immediately BEFORE a symbol's first line, in your own checkout only. Indentation is taken from the symbol's first line and prepended to every non-empty line of text — write the text unindented. Same compare-and-swap as replace_symbol_body: expectedFileHash must match the file's current bytes (get_node answers it as fileHash), a mismatch refuses 'stale' with the fresh hash, and the reply carries the new generation, the anchor node at its shifted lines, and newFileHash.

id.description: Anchor node id — the new lines land before its start line.

expectedFileHash.description: sha256 of the file's current bytes — get_node answers it as fileHash.

text.description: Whole lines to insert, unindented, max 64 KB — the anchor's indentation is applied for you.
